name: Roast Turkey with Stuffing
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 325°F.
2. Thaw the turkey (a 20 lb. turkey will thaw in about 7 days in the refrigerator).
3. Remove packaging and unhook turkey legs from the retainer down by the tail. Then remove the neck from the body cavity.
4. Place the giblet packet into a saucepan, cover with 2-3 cups of water, and simmer for 1 hour or more.
5. Rinse the turkey well and drain, returning it to the refrigerator until needed.
6. After about 30 minutes of giblet cooking, dice celery and onion.
7. Fry sausage, crumbling it as you go, until completely done. Add celery and onion and 1/2 cup water to the frying pan and let simmer for 10 minutes.
8. Using your roasting pan, empty the contents of both boxes of croutons into it.
9. Add the sausage/celery/onion mixture to the roasting pan and stir in the melted butter.
10. Add 1 teaspoon of poultry seasoning and 2 cups of giblet broth.
11. Stir well until the croutons are evenly moistened.
12. Rinse the turkey again, shaking out excess water. Stuff the body cavity loosely (do not pack it tightly; the stuffing will expand during cooking).
13. Put the legs back into the retainer to hold them together and stuff the neck cavity.
14. Fold the wing tips behind the bird's back to help keep the neck skin down so the stuffing won't fall out from the neck.
15. Rinse out the roasting pan and line it with foil coated in vegetable oil.
16. Coat the turkey, all over, with vegetable oil.
17. Place the turkey in the roasting pan.
18. Add any excess stuffing that will fit on top of the turkey legs at the body cavity opening, laying on top.
19. Put the turkey in the oven and roast for approximately 6 1/2 to 7 hours, depending on your oven.
20. Roast until the internal temperature reaches 180°F to 185°F at the thickest part of the thigh (without touching the thermometer to the bone) and the same at the thickest part of the breast.
21. Baste the turkey with a baster every 15 minutes during the last two hours of cooking.
22. If the skin is getting too dark, tent it loosely with foil.
